description_long: This object is a large, multi-barreled rotary rifle resembling a Gatling or minigun, featuring six parallel barrels arranged in a circular cluster at the front. The main body is robust and boxy, with a prominent carrying handle arching over the top and a cylindrical ammunition feed or power pack mounted beneath. The rear section is squared off, with a visible trigger area and a mounting bracket or support structure. The barrels are connected by two circular barrel supports, and the muzzle end is open, showing the individual barrel tips. The overall construction appears heavy-duty, with metallic surfaces and reinforced joints, suitable for sustained fire in urban defense or assault scenarios.
description: A heavy, multi-barreled rotary rifle with a prominent handle, cylindrical ammo feed, and robust metallic construction, resembling a minigun.
description_view_0: Front three-quarter view showing the six-barrel cluster, carrying handle, and cylindrical ammo feed beneath the main body.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the barrels, the boxy main body, and the mounting bracket at the rear.
description_view_2: Rear three-quarter view displaying the squared-off back, handle arch, and the connection between the ammo feed and main body.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focusing on the barrel cluster, handle, and the robust support structure beneath the rifle.
